channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Celebrating the Life and Cultural Legacy of Maria ‘Podesta’ McCloy
item.description: Maria 'Podesta' McCloy, who passed away on 12 May 2026 in Johannesburg, was a vibrant force in South African cultural life. From her early days at Rhodes University studying journalism, she resisted c...

item.title: Public Protector Kholeka Gcaleka Faces Criticism Over Statement on Phala Phala Saga
item.description: Public Protector Kholeka Gcaleka, who has largely maintained a low profile compared to her predecessor Busisiwe Mkhwebane, has sparked controversy this week. Following the Constitutional Court judgmen...

item.title: Siyakholwa Kuse wins WBC minimumweight title after victory over Melvin Jerusalem
item.description: Siyakholwa Kuse claimed the World Boxing Council (WBC) minimumweight title on Saturday night at Emperors Palace, overcoming a flash knockdown in the 11th round to defeat Melvin Jerusalem. The judges s...

item.title: Defence and Tax Office Lead Public Service Staffing Growth in Federal Budget
item.description: The 2026-27 federal budget has seen a significant reshuffle in Australian Public Service staffing. Defence emerged as the biggest beneficiary, with the portfolio set to grow by over 3,100 roles, bring...

item.title: The hardship of daily life in Kenya amid the cost-of-living crisis
item.description: This opinion piece focuses on the difficult conditions many Kenyans are facing in day-to-day life, especially under the pressure of a high cost of living, weak purchasing power, and broader economic s...

item.title: Kaduna North APC Primary Faces Rejection Over Alleged Irregularities
item.description: Di Abdulazeez Abubakar Kaka Campaign Council don come out sharply against di results of di APC House of Representatives primary wey happen for Kaduna North Federal Constituency on 16 May 2026. Di coun...

item.title: Violence Breaks Out During APC House of Reps Primary in Mushin, Lagos
item.description: On Saturday, wahala burst during the APC primary for Mushin Constituency II in Lagos State, as supporters and party members scatter for safety. The trouble started even before voting began for the Hou...

item.title: Trump announces renovations to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool amid controversy
item.description: Former President Donald Trump has shared updates on the ongoing renovations to the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington, D.C., drawing attention for an unusual comparison of its length to th...

item.title: Children’s Creative Birdhouses in Cambodia Brought to Life from Their Drawings
item.description: In Siem Reap, Cambodia, designer Taekhan Yun conducted a unique workshop inviting ninety children to design birdhouses without any constraints or prior architectural knowledge. The project encouraged ...

item.title: Why websites increasingly require human verification to combat bots
item.description: As online bots become more sophisticated, websites are increasingly requiring users to prove they are human. CAPTCHA systems, originally simple tasks like recognising distorted text, have evolved into...

item.title: FCMB Launches Zero-interest Loan Program to Support Women Entrepreneurs
item.description: First City Monument Bank (FCMB) don open new round for SheVentures programme wey dey offer zero-interest loans to women wey dey run their own business. This loan fit reach N10 million, depending on yo...

item.title: Louisiana Senate Primary Highlights Trump-Backed Challenge to Senator Cassidy
item.description: The Louisiana Senate primary on Saturday is shaping up as a significant test for former President Donald Trump, who has endorsed Rep. Julia Letlow in an effort to unseat incumbent Republican Sen. Bill...

item.title: Oyo State Government Pledges Support for Security Agencies, Launches Violent Crime Response Unit
item.description: Oyo State Governor, Seyi Makinde, don assure say him government go continue dey support Nigeria Police Force and all other security agencies for Oyo State to fit protect lives and property well. Dis s...

item.title: Rajouri families suffer food poisoning after consuming wild vegetable, one dead and seven hospitalised
item.description: In a tragic incident in Rajouri district of Jammu and Kashmir, a 60-year-old man lost his life and seven others, including two young children, were admitted to hospital after reportedly consuming a wi...

item.title: Lekompo Artist Lehlogonolo Chauke Appears in Polokwane Court as Trial Date Pending
item.description: Lekompo musician Lehlogonolo ‘Shebeshxt’ Chauke returned to the Polokwane regional court on Friday, with the court set to determine the date for his upcoming trial. Chauke, who has previously been den...

item.title: St. Albert historic sites reopen for public visits this spring and summer
item.description: St. Albert’s historic sites, an essential feature of Alberta’s prairie landscape, have reopened for the spring and summer season, welcoming visitors after months of winter closure. These sites offer a...

item.title: Fire at Fife landfill site continues into fifth day as crews work to extinguish flames
item.description: A fire at the Ladybank landfill site in Fife has persisted into its fifth day, with three fire crews and specialist equipment still battling to extinguish it. The blaze began on Tuesday afternoon, and...

item.title: Cineplex and TSN to Broadcast Select FIFA World Cup 2026 Matches in Canadian Theatres
item.description: As Canada prepares to co-host the 2026 FIFA World Cup alongside the United States and Mexico, Cineplex has announced a partnership with TSN to show select matches in movie theatres across the country....

item.title: Historical Photographs of Tennessee During the American Civil War
item.description: Tennessee played a crucial and turbulent role during the American Civil War, becoming the second most contested state after Virginia. Its strategic rivers, railroads, and geographic location earned it...

item.title: Burundi President Alleges Rwanda's Hostile Actions Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Burundi President Évariste Ndayishimiye, who is also the current African Union president, has openly accused Rwanda of having malicious intentions towards Burundi. This comes in the context of rising ...
